summary: The diff implements the vast majority of the build requirements. All major sections (Hero, About, Services, Testimonials, Downloads, Contact, Footer, Header, WhatsApp button, Payment page, OTP modal) are present, along with the Motoko backend. However, the backend uses a mutable List without stable storage declaration, which may not satisfy the stable var persistence requirement. Several extra components were added that were not requested.

missing_requirements.requirement: User data (name, email, phone) persists in stable storage — store records in a stable var array or HashMap
missing_requirements.reason: The Motoko backend uses `let users = List.empty<User>()` which is NOT declared as `stable var`. This means user data will not survive canister upgrades, violating the stable storage requirement.

hallucinated_features.description: Extra components AksharPhonics.tsx, AksharTuitions.tsx, LearningStrategies.tsx, PhonicsActivitiesDemo.tsx, ProgramOverview.tsx were added
hallucinated_features.reason: These component files are listed as modified or added but were not requested in the build spec. The spec only calls for specific sections: Hero, About, Services, Testimonials, Downloads, Contact, Footer, Header, Payment, OTP Modal, and WhatsApp button.

hallucinated_features.description: OTP expiry time-based validation with a 5-minute window was implemented in the backend
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request only asked for OTP generation, storage, and correct/incorrect verification. A time-based expiry was not specified in any requirement or acceptance criterion.

hallucinated_features.description: Backend marks OTP as verified and clears the OTP field by adding a new user record on successful verification
hallucinated_features.reason: The spec only requires verifyOTP to return true or false. The extra behavior of clearing the OTP and adding a duplicate verified record was not requested.
